subject: Biology
title: Orchids: Masters of Deception
description: Learn Orchids: Masters of Deception in one minute.

hook.text: Orchids are the largest family of flowering plants—over 28,000 species across every continent except Antarctica.

buildup.text: Many orchids trick pollinators with fake nectar, false scents, or mimicked insect shapes.

discovery.text: The bucket orchid traps bees, dunks them in liquid, and forces them past pollen on the only exit.

twist.text: Some orchid seeds are microscopic and contain no food reserve—they rely on fungi to germinate.

climax.text: Darwin was obsessed with orchids. He called their pollination strategies 'the most remarkable' in nature.

quiz.question: How many species of orchids exist?
